patches.hypothesis: The root cause of the overfitting issue was the absence of proper null checks and edge case handling in the original patch. The refined patch addresses these issues by including a more robust approach to handle null values in the headerRecord array, adding more test cases to cover edge cases and general cases, and refactoring the code to make it more modular and reusable.
patches.original_hypothesis: The error was caused by the null value in the headerRecord array, which was not handled correctly in the for loop. The code tried to call the trim() method on a null object, resulting in a NullPointerException.

patches.hypothesis: The method was not checking for null values in the header record, leading to a NullPointerException when encountering an empty header.
patches.changes:
- final boolean emptyHeader = header.trim().isEmpty();
+ final boolean emptyHeader = header == null || header.trim().isEmpty();
patches.diff:
- final boolean emptyHeader = header.trim().isEmpty();
+ final boolean emptyHeader = header == null || header.trim().isEmpty();
